description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a green fuselage and wings adorned with pink cherry blossom motifs and stylized Asian script. The aircraft features two sets of straight, parallel wings supported by wooden struts and cross-bracing wires, giving it a classic early 20th-century appearance. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer with a red and white sunburst pattern and a horizontal stabilizer, both supported by a lightweight frame. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a visible seat and control area. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall construction appears to be a mix of wood, fabric, and metal, typical of early aviation designs.
description: A green, cherry blossom-decorated vintage biplane with open cockpit, wooden struts, and fixed landing gear. The tail features a red sunburst motif and the wings display stylized script.
description_view_0: Side view showing the layered biplane wings, open cockpit, and detailed cherry blossom artwork along the upper wing and fuselage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dual stacked wings, wooden struts, and the open cockpit area behind the propeller.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail's sunburst design, wing bracing, and the stylized script on the lower wing.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail assembly, open cockpit, and the symmetrical structure of the biplane's wings and struts.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings mounted one above the other, supported by wooden struts and cross-bracing; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer; tail features a decorative sunburst pattern.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
